zh.lifeSummary: 當Catherine of Valois, Queen Consort of England, Princess of France於1401年10月27日出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲時，她的父親Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France32歲，母親Isabeau de Bavière32歲。她於1420年6月2日與Henry V King Of England在法國莫瑟勒奧布結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1437年1月3日在英國英格蘭素瑞貝爾蒙享年35歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭米德萊可維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Catherine of Valois, Queen Consort of England, Princess of France于1401年10月27日出生在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲时，她的父亲Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France32岁，母亲Isabeau de Bavière32岁。她于1420年6月2日与Henry V King Of England在法国莫瑟勒奧布结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1437年1月3日在英国英格兰素瑞貝爾蒙享年35岁，埋葬在英国英格兰米德萊可維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Queen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: shortened form of   McQueen  .

English: from the Middle English female personal name Quene, sometimes a pet form of Quenill (Old English Cwēnhild, from cwēn ‘queen’ + hild ‘battle’). Early examples of the name are mainly from southern England, especially Sussex, but its currency in late medieval northern England has also been suggested by records from Yorkshire.
English: possibly a nickname from Middle English quene ‘woman; harlot; crone’ (Old English cwēn), but this is less likely than 2 to have given rise to a hereditary surname.

variants.name: McQueen
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Shuibhne ‘son of Suibhne’ a byname meaning ‘pleasant’. Compare McSweeney .

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Shuain, a Gaelic patronymic based on Old Norse sveinn ‘boy, servant’, which was also used as a personal name (see Swain ). This is given as the origin of the McQueen or McSwan family of Skye, although the spellings are hard to distinguish. Compare Whan .
Americanized form of French   Moquin  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: England,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

variants.name: English
meanings: English, Scottish, and Welsh: ethnic name from Middle English English, Inglish, Inglis ‘English’ (Old English Englisc), sometimes alternating with Anglo-Norman French Engleis, Engles, Anglais, Angles (Old French Englois). Compare Inglis and England . Among the aristocracy and upper gentry it marked out a man of English ancestry from one of Norman or continental origin. In counties bordering England with Scotland and Wales the name distinguished an Englishman from a Scot or a native Welshman on both sides of the border. The name may also have been acquired by English merchants who traded abroad or who lived and worked in a ‘French’ borough in England (one exclusively administered by Normans).
Irish: in Ireland, this name was used to denote an Englishman, often being adopted for Irish Aingléis ‘Englishman’ or through mistranslation for Mac an Ghallóglaigh, see Gallogly and Golightly .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: France
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from France (see 2 below).

French: habitational name given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris); or, in southern France, an ethnic name for someone from what is now the northern half of the country, where the langue d'oïl (Old French as distinct from Occitan) was spoken. Compare   Defrance   and   Lafrance  .
Czech (Franče): from a pet form of František, Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Defrance
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Henri, Jean-Paul.

French (mainly northern): habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris). Compare France .

en.lifeSummary: When Catherine of Valois, Queen Consort of England, Princess of France was born on 27 October 1401, in Paris, Île-de-France, France, her father, Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France, was 32 and her mother, Isabeau de Bavière, was 32. She married Henry V King Of England on 2 June 1420, in Aube, Moselle, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 3 January 1437, in Bermondsey, Surrey, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 35, and was buried in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Catherine of Valois, Queen Consort of England, Princess of France est née le 27 octobre 1401, à Paris, Île-de-France, France, son père, Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Isabeau de Bavière, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Henry V King Of England le 2 juin 1420, à Aube, Moselle, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 3 janvier 1437, à Bermondsey, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 35 ans, et a été enterrée à Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Catherine of Valois, Queen Consort of England, Princess of France am 27. Oktober 1401 in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France 32 und ihre Mutter Isabeau de Bavière 32. Sie hat am 2. Juni 1420 in Alben, Moselle, Frankreich Henry V King Of England geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 3. Januar 1437 im Alter von 35 Jahren in Bermondsey, Surrey, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Catherine of Valois, Queen Consort of England, Princess of France è nata il il 27 ottobre 1401 nel Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, sua padre, Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Isabeau de Bavière, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato Henry V King Of England il 2 giugno 1420, nel Aube, Moselle, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 3 gennaio 1437, nel Bermondsey, Surrey, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 35 anni, e fu sepolta a Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Catherine of Valois, Queen Consort of England, Princess of Franceが1401年10月27日にフランスiレ-デ-フランスパーイスで生まれた時，父Charles Le Fou VI Roi de Franceは32歳，母Isabeau de Bavièreは32歳であった。1420年6月2日にフランスモセルアウベでHenry V King Of Englandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1437年1月3日にイギリスイギリススリーバーモンドセイで35歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスターウエストミンスター アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Catherine of Valois, Queen Consort of England, Princess of France(이)가 1401년 10월 27일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabeau de Bavière(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1420년 6월 2일 프랑스 모셀 오브에서 Henry V King Of England(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1437년 1월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 수리 버몽시에서 35세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Catherine of Valois, Queen Consort of England, Princess of France nasceu em 27 de outubro de 1401, em Paris, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Isabeau de Bavière, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry V King Of England em 2 de junho de 1420, em Aube, Moselle, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 3 de janeiro de 1437, em Bermondsey, Surrey, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 35 anos, e foi sepultada em Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Catherine of Valois, Queen Consort of England, Princess of France родилась 27 октября 1401 г. в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Isabeau de Bavière, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry V King Of England 2 июня 1420 г. в местности ауб, моселл, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 3 января 1437 г. в местности бирмондси, сурри, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 35 лет и была похоронена в местности вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Catherine of Valois, Queen Consort of England, Princess of France nació el 27 de octubre de 1401, en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Isabeau de Bavière, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Henry V King Of England el 2 de junio de 1420, en Aube, Moselle, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 3 de enero de 1437, en Bermondsey, Surrey, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 35 años, y fue sepultada en Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
